2025-sample-sequencing-run

diana baetscher

2026-04-17

MiSeq run on 2026-04-13 using a 2x300 cycle kit.

# preliminaries to get cutadapt going
(base) AKCJM140-DB23:cutadapt_mismatches diana.baetscher$ conda activate cutadaptenv

DATA=/Users/diana.baetscher/git-repos/HAPO/data/20260413_HAPOeDNA/rawdata

# below, the first set of () creates an array, containing n elements of the desired trimmed and unique names
NAMELIST=$(ls ${DATA} | sed 's/e*_L001.*//' | uniq)
echo "${NAMELIST}"

# cutadapt remove primers
# unlike in the previous microkit runs, here we have paired-end data that should include both the fwd and reverse primers
# using the -b (anywhere adapter syntax fixed the issue)
for i in ${NAMELIST}; do
    cutadapt --discard-untrimmed -b TACTCCTTGAAAAAGCCCATTGTA -B ATGGTCCTGAAGTAAGAACCAGATG -o trimmed/${i}_R1.fastq.gz -p trimmed/${i}_R2.fastq.gz "$DATA/${i}_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz" "$DATA/${i}_L001_R2_001.fastq.gz" >> cutadapt_out.txt
    done


# then unzip the reads in the /trimmed directory
pigz -d *gz

Notes about the “anywhere” adapter from Cutadapt docs: For this type of adapter, the sequence is specified with -b ADAPTER (or use the longer spelling –anywhere ADAPTER). The adapter may appear in the beginning (even degraded), within the read, or at the end of the read (even partially). The decision which part of the read to remove is made as follows: If there is at least one base before the found adapter, then the adapter is considered to be a 3’ adapter and the adapter itself and everything following it is removed. Otherwise, the adapter is considered to be a 5’ adapter and it is removed from the read, but the sequence after it remains.

Are there empty files? If so, I should move them before they cause trouble:

find /Users/diana.baetscher/git-repos/HAPO/data/20260413_HAPOeDNA/trimmed -type f -empty -exec mv {} /Users/diana.baetscher/git-repos/HAPO/data/20260413_HAPOeDNA/trimmed_empty/ \;

None, presently.

Dada2 and ASV analysis

Launching into the R-portion of the analysis in dada2

library(dada2)
## Warning: package 'dada2' was built under R version 4.2.1
## Loading required package: Rcpp
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.4
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.0
## ✔ purrr     1.0.2
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
# file location
path <- "../data/20260413_HAPOeDNA/trimmed"

list.files(path)

## [337] "e05604-1_S24_R1.fastq"  "e05604-1_S24_R2.fastq"  "e05604-2_S86_R1.fastq" 
## [340] "e05604-2_S86_R2.fastq"  "e05604-3_S148_R1.fastq" "e05604-3_S148_R2.fastq"
## [343] "e05605-1_S36_R1.fastq"  "e05605-1_S36_R2.fastq"  "e05605-2_S98_R1.fastq" 
## [346] "e05605-2_S98_R2.fastq"  "e05605-3_S160_R1.fastq" "e05605-3_S160_R2.fastq"
## [349] "e05606-1_S48_R1.fastq"  "e05606-1_S48_R2.fastq"  "e05606-2_S110_R1.fastq"
## [352] "e05606-2_S110_R2.fastq" "e05606-3_S172_R1.fastq" "e05606-3_S172_R2.fastq"
## [355] "e05607-1_S60_R1.fastq"  "e05607-1_S60_R2.fastq"  "e05607-2_S122_R1.fastq"
## [358] "e05607-2_S122_R2.fastq" "e05607-3_S184_R1.fastq" "e05607-3_S184_R2.fastq"
## [361] "filtered"               "NC-1_S61_R1.fastq"      "NC-1_S61_R2.fastq"     
## [364] "NC-2_S123_R1.fastq"     "NC-2_S123_R2.fastq"     "NC-3_S185_R1.fastq"    
## [367] "NC-3_S185_R2.fastq"     "PC-1_S62_R1.fastq"      "PC-1_S62_R2.fastq"     
## [370] "PC-2_S124_R1.fastq"     "PC-2_S124_R2.fastq"     "PC-3_S186_R1.fastq"    
## [373] "PC-3_S186_R2.fastq"
# paired-end data
fnFs <- sort(list.files(path, pattern = "_R1.fastq", full.names = TRUE))
fnRs <- sort(list.files(path, pattern = "_R2.fastq", full.names = TRUE))
# Extract sample names, assuming filenames have format: SAMPLENAME_XXX.fastq
sample.names <- sapply(strsplit(basename(fnFs), "_"), `[`, 1)
plotQualityProfile(fnFs[1:3])
## Warning: The `<scale>` argument of `guides()` cannot be `FALSE`. Use "none" instead as
## of ggplot2 3.3.4.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the dada2 package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/benjjneb/dada2/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

plotQualityProfile(fnRs[1:3])

These are classic 600-cycle kit patterns, where the forward read is much higher quality at the later cycles than the reverse read. Consequently, let’s trim to 250 in the FWD and 200 in the REV. That should still give us >20 bp of overlap.

# Place filtered files in filtered/ subdirectory
filtFs <- file.path(path, "filtered", paste0(sample.names, "_F_filt.fastq.gz"))
filtRs <- file.path(path, "filtered", paste0(sample.names, "_R_filt.fastq.gz"))
names(filtFs) <- sample.names
names(filtRs) <- sample.names

Change truncLen to see how that affects downstream processing.

out <- filterAndTrim(fnFs, filtFs, fnRs, filtRs, truncLen=c(250,200),
              maxN=0, maxEE=c(2,4), truncQ=2, rm.phix=TRUE,
              compress=TRUE, multithread=TRUE) 
head(out)
##                        reads.in reads.out
## e05548-1_S1_R1.fastq      30729     27420
## e05548-2_S63_R1.fastq     35060     32368
## e05548-3_S125_R1.fastq    40570     36166
## e05549-1_S2_R1.fastq      33953     29630
## e05549-2_S64_R1.fastq     32890     30582
## e05549-3_S126_R1.fastq    35917     29932

Error rates

fwd_list <- as.list(filtFs)
rev_list <- as.list(filtRs)

new_list <- fwd_list[1:10] # smaller amount of data for learning the error rates.
new_list2 <- rev_list[1:10]


# fwd error rates
errF <- learnErrors(new_list, multithread=TRUE)
## 47229000 total bases in 188916 reads from 10 samples will be used for learning the error rates.
# reverse error rates
errR <- learnErrors(new_list2, multithread=TRUE)
## 37783200 total bases in 188916 reads from 10 samples will be used for learning the error rates.
0
## [1] 0
# plot the erors
p1 <- plotErrors(errF, nominalQ=TRUE)
p2 <- plotErrors(errR, nominalQ=TRUE)

Sample inference

# forwards
dadaFs <- dada(filtFs, err=errF, multithread=TRUE)
## Sample 1 - 27420 reads in 5532 unique sequences.

Merge paired end reads

mergers <- mergePairs(dadaFs, filtFs, dadaRs, filtRs, verbose=TRUE)

# Inspect the merger data.frame from the first sample
head(mergers[[1]])
##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     sequence
## 1                        TGTTTATTAAAGCACTACTGTACTACGTCAGTATTAAAAATAGCCTACTCCTAAACATCCCACTACAACTACCATGTATGTACTCACATACTAGGACCCTAGTCTCCCCCATAAATATTTATACATATACACACTATGTATTATTGTGCATTCATTTATTTTCCATACGACTATGTTAAAGCCCGTATTAAAACTTATTAATTTTACAAAATACATAATTTGCACGCTCTTACATATTATACCTCCACTTGTACCTCATATCCATTATATCCTATGGTCGCTCCATTAGATCACGAGCTTAATCACCATGCCGCGTGAAACCAGCAACCCGCTCGGCAGGGATCCCTCTTCTCGCACCGGGCCCATAACCTGTGGGGGTAGCTAATTAATGATCTTTATAAGA
## 2                        TGTTTATTAAAGCACTACTGTACTACGTCAGTATTAAAAATAGCCTACTCCTAAACATCCCACTACAACTACCATGTATGTACTCACATACTAGGACCCTAGTCTCCCCCATAAATATTTATACATATACACACTATGTATTATTGTGCATTCATTTATTTTCCATACGACTATGTTAAAGCCCGTATTAAAACTTATTAATTTTACAAAATACATAATTTGCACGCTCTTACATATTATACCTCCACTTGTACCTCATATCCATTATATCCTATGGCCGCTCCATTAGATCACGAGCTTAATCACCATGCCGCGTGAAACCAGCAACCCGCTCGGCAGGGATCCCTCTTCTCGCACCGGGCCCATAACCTGTGGGGGTAGCTAATTAATGATCTTTATAAGA
## 3                                                                                                                                   AGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATCTACTCCTTGAAAAAGCCCATTGTACCTGCACCGCCAGAAGAAACCCACTGATCGGAACGCTGATAGCAGTAATATCTTGCAGCCTTCATTTGATGCTGTCCTACATCCGTGGCTACTATTGCCTGTCCTTGTGTTTTGTCACTGACTTTTTTGACTACCTGAGCCATTTTGATCGAACCATCTGGTTCTTACTTCAGGACCATAGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCACCAGGAAATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTGAAAAAAA
## 4                        TGTTTATTAAAGCACTACTGTACTACGTCAGTATTAAAAATAGCCTACTCCTAAACATCCCACTACAACTACCATGTATGTACTCACATACTAGGACCCTAGTCTCCCCCATAAATATTTATACATATACACACTATGTATTATTGTGCATTCATTTATTTTCCATACGACTATGTTAAAGCCCGTATTAAAACTTATTAATTTTACAAAATACATAATTTGCACGCTCTTACATATTATACCTCCACTTGTACCTCATATCCATTATATCCTATGGCCGCTCCATTAGATCACGAGCTTAATCACCATGCCGCGTGAAACCAGCAACCCGCTCGGCAGGGATCCCTCTTCTCGCACCGGGCCCATAACTTGTGGGGGTAGCTAATTAATGATCTTTATAAGA
## 5 TGTTTATTAAAGCACTACTGTACTACGTCAGTATTAAAAATAGCCTACTCCTAAACATCCCACTACAACTACCATGTATGTACTCACATACTAGGACCCTAGTCTCCCCCATAAATATTTATACATATACACACTATGTATTATTGTGCATTCATTTATTTTCCATACGACTATGTTAAAGCCCGTATTAAAACTTATTAATTTTACAAAATACATAATTTGCACGCTCTTACATATTATACCTCCACTTGTACCTCATATCCATTATATCCTATGGTCGCTCCATTAGATCACGAGCTTAATCACCATGCCGCGTGAAACCAGCAACCCGCTCGGCAGGGATCCCTCTTCTCGCACCGGGCCCATAACCTGTGGGGGTAGCTAATTAATGATCTTTATAAGACAGCTGGGTCTTACGTCCAGGAC
## 6                                                                                                                                                           CGATTGCCATTTTATTCTCCCTTGGATTCGAGGCGCTTGATGCGGTCCTCGTGATTTTCGGACCGTTCTAAAACAACGGCCAGCTTAAGATTGAGTTCTGAGACATTCTCTGTCAAGTGTTTTAGTTGAGACACACCGTCCCGGAAAATTGTCAAACTCAACGTACCGATTATGACCATCATTGTATCAATCAAAGTAGTTGATAAATGCTCCATAGTCTTAGCCGTCTTGAGTTATGCTGCCGAGCTTCTTTGATAGCCACTTTCCAACCG
##   abundance forward reverse nmatch nmismatch nindel prefer accept
## 1     23927       1       1     47         0      0      2   TRUE
## 2      2943       1       2     47         0      0      1   TRUE
## 3       268       2       3    154         0      0      2   TRUE
## 4       199       1       8     47         0      0      1   TRUE
## 5        19       1       7     24         0      0      1   TRUE
## 6         6       4       4    178         0      0      2   TRUE

Make a sequence table

seqtab <- makeSequenceTable(mergers)
dim(seqtab)
## [1] 186 597
# Inspect distribution of sequence lengths
table(nchar(getSequences(seqtab)))
## 
## 250 252 253 257 259 261 262 263 264 272 273 275 279 281 283 285 286 289 290 292 
##  31  27   1   6   1  26   1   2   1   2   1  11   1   6  13   2   2   1   1   1 
## 293 294 296 298 299 301 302 303 306 313 317 321 332 339 340 342 346 350 351 356 
##   1   1   2   1   1   3   3   1   1   1   1   2   2   1   1   1   1   1   2   1 
## 358 360 365 367 370 376 382 386 387 388 401 402 403 404 407 408 409 415 416 417 
##   1   1   1   1   1   2   1   1   1   2  59   1 300   1   2   5   1   2   2   2 
## 418 420 421 422 423 424 425 426 427 429 436 
##   1  10   9  12   2   2   2   3   2   1   1

Let’s remove the singletons and off-target sequences

# this seems like the obvious size range
# but if necessary, we can investigate different filtering options here
seqtab2 <- seqtab[,nchar(colnames(seqtab)) %in% 401:403]

Remove chimeras

seqtab.nochim <- removeBimeraDenovo(seqtab2, method="consensus", multithread=TRUE, verbose=TRUE)
## Identified 169 bimeras out of 360 input sequences.
dim(seqtab.nochim)
## [1] 186 191

This seems too high… come back to this as well!

Calculate frequency of chimeras

sum(seqtab.nochim)/sum(seqtab2)
## [1] 0.9940853

Track reads through the pipeline

getN <- function(x) sum(getUniques(x))
track <- cbind(out, sapply(dadaFs, getN), sapply(dadaRs, getN), sapply(mergers, getN), rowSums(seqtab.nochim))

colnames(track) <- c("input", "filtered", "denoisedF", "denoisedR", "merged", "nonchim")
rownames(track) <- sample.names
head(track)
##          input filtered denoisedF denoisedR merged nonchim
## e05548-1 30729    27420     27383     27382  27362   27069
## e05548-2 35060    32368     32347     32321  32228   32085
## e05548-3 40570    36166     36140     36077  36037   35734
## e05549-1 33953    29630     29617     29587  29332   29252
## e05549-2 32890    30582     30559     30550  30487   30466
## e05549-3 35917    29932     29915     29881  28229   27262

Export files for taxonomy and samples/ASVs

 #make fasta file with ASVs
    asv_seqs=colnames(seqtab.nochim)
    for(i in 1:length(asv_seqs))
    {
        write.table(paste(">ASV",i, sep=""),file="csv_outputs/hapo_20260413_ASV.csv", append=TRUE, col.names = F, row.names = F, quote=F)
        write.table(paste(asv_seqs[i], sep=""),file="csv_outputs/hapo_20260413_ASV.csv", append=TRUE, col.names = F, row.names = F, quote=F)
    }

That’s the input for the FASTA blastn search.

Make ASV headers correspond to those output in the FASTA file.

# Make map between brief names and full sequences
briefToSeq <- colnames(seqtab.nochim)
names(briefToSeq) <- paste0("ASV", seq(ncol(seqtab.nochim))) # Seq1, Seq2, ...
# Make new sequence table with brief names
st.brief <- seqtab.nochim
colnames(st.brief) <- names(briefToSeq)

# export the pool seq table with brief names:
write.csv(st.brief, file="csv_outputs/hapo_20260413_sampleTable.csv")

Sample info

tmp <- as.data.frame(st.brief)
sample_df <- rownames_to_column(tmp, var = "sample")

long_df <- sample_df %>%
  pivot_longer(2:length(sample_df)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(reads = value, ASV = name) 

# long_df %>%
#   write_csv("csv_outputs/microkit_samples_20260324.csv")

long_df %>%
  filter(reads > 1) %>% # pretty minimal read filter
  ggplot(aes(x = sample, y = reads, fill = ASV)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0))

# ggsave("pdf_outputs/microkit_20260324_quicklook.png", width = 10, height = 4)

Use the custom blast database on Sedna to assign haplotypes for these data.

This was a little clunky for some reason…

blastn -db hapo_CR -query /home/dbaetscher/workdir/blast/HAPO_eDNA/hapo_20260413_ASV.csv -out CR_2025_results.txt -outfmt 6
blast_results <- read_tsv("../data/blast_outputs/CR_2025_results.txt", col_names = F) 
## Rows: 11078 Columns: 12
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr  (2): X1, X2
## dbl (10): X3, X4, X5, X6, X7, X8, X9, X10, X11, X12
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
blast_data <- blast_results %>%
  dplyr::rename(ASV = X1, ref_id = X2, perc_id = X3, len = X4, mismatch = X5, gap = X6)

top_hits <- blast_data %>%
  group_by(ASV) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = perc_id, n = 1, with_ties = T)
top_hits %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ref_id, y = ASV)) +
  geom_tile() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)
  )

How many of the ASVs only match one CR?

top_hits %>%
  add_tally(name = "n_CR_seqs") %>%
  filter(n_CR_seqs > 1)
## # A tibble: 98 × 13
## # Groups:   ASV [43]
##    ASV   ref_id perc_id   len mismatch   gap    X7    X8    X9   X10   X11   X12
##    <chr> <chr>    <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
##  1 ASV1  CR01     100     379        0     0     1   379    18   396     0   701
##  2 ASV1  CR31     100     377        0     0     3   379    20   396     0   697
##  3 ASV1… CR17      97.1   379        9     1     1   377    18   396     0   638
##  4 ASV1… CR08      97.1   379        9     1     1   377    18   396     0   638
##  5 ASV1… CR01     100     376        0     0     1   376    18   393     0   695
##  6 ASV1… CR31     100     374        0     0     3   376    20   393     0   691
##  7 ASV1… CR01     100     379        0     0     1   379    18   396     0   701
##  8 ASV1… CR31     100     377        0     0     3   379    20   396     0   697
##  9 ASV1… CR40      99.5   379        2     0     1   379    18   396     0   689
## 10 ASV1… CR13      99.5   379        2     0     1   379    18   396     0   689
## # ℹ 88 more rows
## # ℹ 1 more variable: n_CR_seqs <int>

43 ASVs that supposedly match more than one CR haplotype and 148 ASVs that only match a single CR haplotype.

I could also be more stringent about mismatches.

Look at it the other way, how many ASVs match a given CR haplotype

long_df %>%
  left_join(., top_hits) %>%
  filter(reads > 1) %>%
  select(ASV, ref_id, perc_id, mismatch) %>%
  unique() %>%
  filter(mismatch < 2) %>%
  group_by(ref_id) %>%
  #tally(name = "n_ASV") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ref_id, y = ASV, fill = perc_id)) +
  geom_tile() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8)
  )
## Joining with `by = join_by(ASV)`
## Warning in left_join(., top_hits): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 1 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.

ggsave("pdf_outputs/2025_samples_20260413.png", width = 8, height = 11)
